ddls:
CREATE TABLE "universities" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each university or college */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the university or college */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the university or college is located */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the university or college is located */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the university or college is located */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the university or college is located */,
  "school_type" TEXT /* Type of university or college (public/private) */,
  "school_size" TEXT /* Size of the university or college (small/medium/large) */,
  "established_year" INTEGER /* Year the university or college was established */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the university or college */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_data" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary data point */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the universities table */,
  "degree_level" TEXT /* Level of degree (bachelor's/master's/doctoral) */,
  "major" TEXT /* Major or field of study */,
  "starting_median_salary" TEXT /* Median starting salary of graduates */,
  "mid_career_median_salary" TEXT /* Median mid-career salary of graduates */,
  "mid_career_10th_percentile_salary" TEXT /* 10th percentile mid-career salary of graduates */,
  "mid_career_25th_percentile_salary" TEXT /* 25th percentile mid-career salary of graduates */,
  "mid_career_75th_percentile_salary" TEXT /* 75th percentile mid-career salary of graduates */,
  "mid_career_90th_percentile_salary" TEXT /* 90th percentile mid-career salary of graduates */,
  "salary_year" INTEGER /* Year the salary data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the region is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "university_regions" (
  "university_region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each university-region pair */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the universities table */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the regions table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "data_year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)
